reasoning: The 1-year real interest rate series calculated by the Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland (REAINTRATREARAT1YE) is highly volatile month-to-month. Over the past 12 months, the series has experienced 7 increases and 5 decreases, showing almost a random walk with slight mean-reverting tendencies. The most recent value for February 2026 is 0.885, which is somewhat below the historical 1-year average of ~1.10. Due to this being below the recent mean, there is a slightly elevated probability of the next monthly movement being an increase due to mean reversion. Combined with the baseline historical frequency of month-over-month increases (around 50-55%), I estimate the probability of an increase by March 2026 to be 53%.
